guideline_references: ASCIA Acute Management of Anaphylaxis Guidelines 2023 (used to EXCLUDE anaphylaxis)
intentional_test_features: Zebra recognition: a rare neuroendocrine syndrome whose acute exacerbation (flushing + secretory diarrhoea + new bronchospasm) closely mimics anaphylaxis and acute asthma — the test is whether the system recognises a systemic, recurrent, multi-system vasomotor pattern rather than anchoring on the obvious mimic.

Anchoring / premature-closure trap: the presentation screams anaphylaxis, but the recurrent episodic history, absence of allergen/urticaria, chronic right-heart features and weight loss point elsewhere.
Dangerous error of commission: reflexively giving/advising ADRENALINE (as for anaphylaxis) can precipitate a catecholamine-driven carcinoid crisis — the case probes whether the model avoids this.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person out-of-hours home visit where the clinician auscultated a tricuspid murmur, measured vitals and gave salbutamol via spacer; the AI Doctor cannot examine, auscultate, measure vitals without a device, give a drug, obtain urinary 5-HIAA/echocardiography or confirm the diagnosis remotely — it can recognise the pattern and escalate.
Age and sex inferred (no signal in source) — flagged.
Source category: EMD (Endocrine & Metabolic Disorders), source note EMD-005.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
